title: Lively Briny Mexican Pumpkin Tacos
description: A vibrant and energizing Mexican dish featuring roasted poblano peppers, crunchy pumpkin seeds, and a zesty briny marinade.
instructions: Toss the poblano peppers with 2 tbsp of oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes or until the skin is blistered and charred.
While the peppers are roasting, heat 1 tbsp of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red onion and cook for 5-7 minutes or until softened, stirring occasionally.
Add the pumpkin seeds to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until toasted and fragrant.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool in a bowl covered with plastic wrap.
Once cool enough to handle, peel off the skin, remove the seeds, and chop the flesh.
In a blender or food processor, combine the chopped peppers, chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and coriander. Blend until smooth.
Heat the lime juice in a skillet over low heat.
Add the blended pepper mixture to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked onion and pumpkin seeds to the skillet and stir to combine.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the pepper mixture onto a tortilla, topping with queso fresco, and garnishing with fresh cilantro.
